stories.title: Fraud accusations fly as Hungarians vote in contentious election
stories.summary: BUDAPEST — Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán’s camp and the opposition are accusing each other of electoral fraud as both sides gear up for a potential clash over the results of Sunday’s crucial election. Orbán is facing an unprecedented challenge to his 16-year rule in Budapest as challenger Péter Magyar, a former government insider, and his Tisza party were leading in the polls going into Sunday’s vote . Some 54 percent of registered voters had cast their ballots by 1 p.m

stories.title: Hungarians vote in hard-fought election that could oust Viktor Orbán after 16 years
stories.summary: Rightwing leader trails in polls to Péter Magyar, despite support from JD Vance on recent visit Hungarians are heading to the ballot boxes to vote in a landmarkparliamentary election that could oust Viktor Orbán after 16 years in power and potentially reshape the central European country’s relations with the EU, Moscow and Washington. During the campaign, Orbán – the EU’s longest-serving leader – has trailed in the polls as he faces an unprecedented challenge from Péter Magyar, a former elite me

stories.title: Russian drone attacks persist despite Kremlin’s Easter ceasefire, Ukrainian forces say
stories.summary: Ukraine reports 469 violations of Putin’s 32-hour ceasefire, hours after deadly drone attacks on Odesa and Kherson Russia continued to strike Ukrainian positions with drones after a Kremlin-declared Easter ceasefire took effect on Saturday, a Ukrainian military officer said. “The ceasefire is not being observed by the Russian side,” said Serhii Kolesnychenko, a communications officer for the 148th Separate Artillery Brigade. Continue reading...

stories.title: Is this the end of Viktor Orbán’s regime? - podcast
stories.summary: “Hungary has been a model for the Trump presidency for a while now,” the Guardian journalist Flora Garamvolgyi tells Helen Pidd. “And US Republicans looked at Hungary for these past years as a model to follow. “[Viktor] Orbán is currently on his fourth consecutive term. And the fact that he has been so successful and he had similar narrative, similar ideologies to US Republicans in terms of immigration, for example, I think they have found a link to connect with Orbán and they were studying his

stories.title: How Hungary's knife-edge election could impact the US and Russia
stories.summary: The BBC's Europe Editor Katya Adler reports from Prime Minister Victor Orbán's home town of Felscút.

stories.title: Record turnout in Hungary’s pivotal vote
stories.summary: A record number of voters cast their ballots in the first hours of Sunday’s key election in Hungary, which could potentially spell the end of Prime Minister Viktor Orbán’s 16-year rule. According to data from the Hungarian national election office , 54 percent of registered voters had cast their ballots by 1 p.m., marking the highest turnout since at least 2010, when Orbán first took power. In the previous parliamentary election in 2022, turnout stood at 40 percent at the same time. !funct

stories.title: Explosives found near pipeline in Serbia probably ‘Russian provocation’, says expert
stories.summary: Former Ukrainian major general says 4kg of material was most likely an attempt to influence Hungary’s election Hungary elections explainer The amount of explosives discovered in Serbia last week would not have been enough to destroy the Balkan Stream gas pipeline, prompting an expert to conclude it was probably a Russian intelligence plot aimed at influencing Hungary’s impending election. A former Ukrainian major general and a munitions specialist told the Guardian calculations made by his compa

stories.title: ‘I didn’t want to be on medication the rest of my life’: veteran runs psilocybin retreats for PTSD before FDA approval
stories.summary: Researchers say ‘magic mushrooms’ can help with traumatic symptoms, but urge caution as states expand access Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox After three combat deployments in Afghanistan, during which he suffered traumatic brain injuries from concussive blasts, army ranger Jesse Gould developed post-traumatic stress disorder and said he “drank almost every night to cope”. In times of hardship, veterans sometimes turn to “medication and talk therapy,

stories.title: Hungary election: Orban faces tight race in pivotal vote
stories.summary: Hungarians are heading to the polls in an election that could end Prime Minister Viktor Orban's 16-year rule. Opposition leader Peter Magyar has emerged as the biggest threat to Orban's grip on power.

stories.title: Record turnout expected as parliamentary election in Hungary gets under way
stories.summary: Polls opened at 6am across the country, and will close 7pm local time. So far, all figures point to a record turnout.

stories.title: Hungarians vote in big numbers on whether to end Orbán rule and elect rival
stories.summary: Most polls favour Péter Magyar, who fronts a grassroots party, and early figures point to a record turnout of voters.

stories.title: Human rights groups decry US plan for Guantánamo camp for Cuban migrants
stories.summary: Exclusive: Dozens of organizations write to Congress after general announced plan to ‘deal with’ those fleeing any humanitarian crisis on the island Dozens of US and international human rights organizations are decrying the Trump administration ’s plans to establish a migrant “camp” for fleeing Cubans at the Guantánamo Bay military base if the island nation’s crisis worsens under pressure from the US, according to a letter to members of Congress on Friday. The 85 groups plan to submit the joint

stories.title: How much sway does Trump have over Hungarian voters?
stories.summary: US President Trump has backed Hungarian PM Orban. It's unclear whether the endorsement will help in Hungary's election — the European hard-right has struggled with Trump's recent anti-Europe policies and threats.

stories.title: 54,14 százalék a részvételi arány délután 1-ig, megérkeztek a fővárosi választók
stories.summary: Ez hét százalékponttal magasabb, mint az eddigi rekord. Orbán Viktor szerint a nagyon sok szavazó egy dolgot jelent. Kövesse a választás napját a Telexen percről percre!

stories.title: Volt már olyan szavazás április 12-én, amikor élesen bírálták Orbán Viktort
stories.summary: Brüsszel és Moszkva már 23 éve is elhangzott egy mondatban, amikor Magyarország európai uniós csatlakozásáról tartottak ügydöntő népszavazást.

stories.title: Az egész világ a magyar választásra figyel
stories.summary: A külföldi lapok az egész jobboldali-populista mozgalom szempontjából sorsszerűként jellemzik a magyar választást, mindenki nemzetközi szinten is messzemenő következtetéseket vonna le az eredményből.

stories.title: Voting begins in Hungary with record turnout in first hour of high-stakes election
stories.summary: If the race ends up being too close to call, the final result may not be known for several days after polls close.

stories.title: Orbán Viktor nem üzent Magyar Péternek, de gratulálna neki, ha nyernének
stories.summary: Orbán Viktor a szavazása utáni sajtótájékoztatón visszakérdezett, hogy mennyi is a részvételi arány, azután pedig kérdezték Orbán Gáspár jelenlétéről Csádban, és arról is, hogy van-e valami az elmúlt 16 évben, amit megbánt.

stories.title: Volt MTVA-s vezető: Nem vagyok tiszás, de a rendszerváltásra szavazok
stories.summary: Bognár Tibor a nemzeti érdek ügyében Moszkvába telefonáló Szijjártóról és félő MTVA-s dolgozókról is beszél a felvételen.

stories.title: Orbán: I came to win
stories.summary: After casting his vote, the leader of Fidesz briefly answered questions from the press.

stories.title: Magyar: Nobody can seriously expect that the Tisza Party won't win
stories.summary: Commenting on the expected record turnout, the chairman of the Tisza Party said that he believes there may be polling stations where even one or two votes could make a difference.

stories.title: Magyar Péter szavazott, majd közölte, két ciklusban maximálják a miniszterelnöki hivatalt, ha nyernek
stories.summary: A Tisza Párt elnöke nagy újságírói érdeklődés mellett tartott sajtótájékoztatót Hegyvidéken.

stories.title: Egy brazil rendőrkutya 48 tonna marihuánát szagolt ki
stories.summary: A fogást öt órán át pakolt több tucatnyi rendőr, és négy teherautó kellett elszállítására.

stories.title: Budapesten kétharmadával, a legkisebb településeken felével nőtt a részvétel
stories.summary: A választási hajlandóság minden településtípus esetében megugrott 2022-höz viszonyítva, ám a legkisebb községekben a növekedés mértéke elmaradt a többi településhez képest.

stories.title: Tiborcz István üzlettársa levélben szólította fel a Bayer Construct munkatársait, hogy szavazzanak a Fideszre
stories.summary: Az érzelmes levél szerint az, aki nem a Fideszre szavaz, távozhat a cégtől.

stories.title: Balogh Levente: Nem tartom üzletembernek Mészáros Lőrincet
stories.summary: A Szentkirályi Ásványvíz alapítója és tulajdonosa azt mondta a Telex élő műsorában, hogy nagyon bátor embernek tartja Magyar Pétert.

stories.title: Mit gondol, hány hírt adott ki az MTI a kampányhajrában Orbán Viktorról? Na és Magyar Péterről?
stories.summary: Annyit segítünk, hogy majdnem tízszeres a különbség a kettő között.

stories.title: A kormánymédia már ott tart, hogy a Tisza a választás estéjén Majdanná változtatná Budapestet, Magyar szerint ez csak fideszes rémhír
stories.summary: Egy „kiszivárgott belsős akcióterv” alapján arról cikkeznek, vereség esetén a Tisza szervezetten megindulna a kormányzati épületek ellen, Magyar Péter szerint ez csak „erőtlen és kétségbeesett hangulatkeltés”.

stories.title: Rácz András: Legalább három orosz kormányközeli szervről tudunk, ami beavatkozik a magyar választásokba
stories.summary: Az Oroszország-szakértő szerint elképzelhető, hogy a Fidesz nem fogja elfogadni a választás eredményét.

stories.title: An environmental disaster in Moldova has Russia’s fingerprints all over it | Paula Erizanu
stories.summary: The Ukraine war on our doorstep is a constant threat. Contaminated drinking water is a dangerous new twist In the second week of March, the nature vlogger Ilie Cojocari went out to film the arrival of spring on the Nistru (Dniester) river, 70 metres away from his home in Naslavcea, a village bordering Ukraine on the northernmost point of Moldova. But as he approached the river he could smell the stench of oil rising up from the water and see dark spots floating on its surface. Something was wron

stories.title: ‘He cares about Hungarians’: the small Ukrainian town divided over Orbán
stories.summary: The rightwing populist’s support for the majority-Hungarian population of Berehove means they may offer their votes in return Hungary elections: what is at stake and who is likely to win? Across much of Ukraine, Sunday’s parliamentary election in Hungary is being followed with a singular hope: that Viktor Orbán, the Kremlin-friendly leader who has made opposition to Kyiv a centrepiece of his campaign, will be voted out after 16 years in office. But in Berehove, the mood is more complicated. Cont

stories.title: As Hungary gears up for a historic election, DW answers a few key questions
stories.summary: Hungary's election on April 12 is the most important for the state since 1989, and also of historic significance for the EU. DW takes a more detailed look.

stories.title: Hungary Election: An Orbán victory would be good news for Europe
stories.summary: Hungary elections: An Orbán victory would be good news for Europe, writes Gerolf Annemans of the “Patriots for Europe” group in the European Parliament in an op-ed for Euronews. Annemans says Orbán has become a symbol of resistance to centralisation.

stories.title: „Először vagyok fideszes rendezvényen, de megfélemlítve érzem magam”
stories.summary: Sokan voltak a fideszes kampányzárón a Várnegyedben, de nem mindenki azért ment, hogy Orbánt hallgassa. A fideszes országjárást végigkísérő csapat tagjai jól kitűntek a tömegből.

stories.title: A lengyel hírszerzés volt vezetője szerint nevén kell nevezni a dolgokat, Orbán és Szijjártó orosz ügynökök
stories.summary: Andrzej Derlatka azt sem teljesen érti, Szijjártó miért jár annyit Belaruszba.

stories.title: Aleksandar Vučić Orbánnak: Még ha veszítesz is, örökké hálásak leszünk
stories.summary: A szerb elnök mellett Robert Fico, Szlovákia miniszterelnöke is üzent Orbán Viktornak. Azt kívánta neki, „a parlamenti választásokon teljesüljön minden politikai célja”.

stories.title: Akkor vége van
stories.summary: De csak a kampánynak. Vasárnap az eddig legintenzívebb választási időszak után Magyarország választ. A korábbi kampányok után újdonság volt, hogy a legfontosabb események az utcákon és a tereken zajlottak, és hogy Magyar Péter után most a magát „street fighterként” azonosító Orbán Viktor is utcára ment. Fotósaink legjobb felvételeiből válogattunk.

stories.title: Hungary: Scandal, fear and hope ahead of vote
stories.summary: Fake news and a growing number of political scandals: Hungary hasn't seen an election this fraught since after 1989's collapse of the communist dictatorship.

stories.title: Hungary Elections: There is hope that Peter Magyar can deliver a new beginning
stories.summary: What will Hungary’s elections bring? In an op-ed for Euronews, EU politician Valérie Hayer expresses hope that Péter Magyar could usher in a new political beginning, while warning that another term for Viktor Orbán risks cementing irreversible democratic backsliding.

stories.title: Even if Victor Orbán is ousted on Sunday, Hungary’s return to liberal democracy is not guaranteed | Gabriela Greilinger and Cas Mudde
stories.summary: Challenger Péter Magyar is no progressive – and after 16 years of creeping authoritarianism, the PM has embedded Fidesz in the Hungarian state On Sunday, Hungarians will go to the polls to decide on their country’s direction for the next four years in an election that looks as if it will be a nail-biter. Viktor Orbán, Europe’s longest-serving prime minister – who has been in power for 16 years and transformed his country into an electoral autocracy – could lose the election . Ahead of the vote,

stories.title: Fact check: Orban points to Ukraine over pipeline bomb
stories.summary: Orban strongly suggests explosives found near a key gas pipeline in Serbia are evidence of Ukrainian sabotage. Serbia's intelligence chief publicly disagrees.

stories.title: Hungary election: Voters want new EU approach, but tough Ukraine stance, poll shows
stories.summary: A new poll ahead of Hungary's election suggests that most voters want at least minor changes to ties with the EU. But it also found skepticism of financial support for Ukraine, and voters prioritizing domestic issues.

stories.title: Poland: "Frog Patrol" saves thousands of amphibians during migration
stories.summary: Volunteers near Warsaw form a frog patrol each spring, helping thousands of frogs and toads cross a road safely and protect local breeding grounds.

stories.title: Elections in Hungary: What do the polls say?
stories.summary: Medián, currently considered among the most accurate pollsters in Hungary, predicts a two-thirds majority in parliament for opposition party Tisza. Most other pollsters estimate a smaller lead for them, while few have the ruling Fidesz party narrowly ahead.

stories.title: A new generation of politicians of colour is emerging in France. The backlash speaks volumes
stories.summary: Local elections have led to a surge of racism in a country that still struggles to see itself as anything other than white Saint-Denis is just over 9km from the centre of Paris but is in the poorest department in all of metropolitan France, a region marked by unemployment, low incomes and social disadvantage. But Saint Denis’s town hall was the backdrop to memorably joyous celebrations on the evening of 15 March. A delirious crowd carried the new mayor shoulder high, chanting his name over and o

stories.title: Virtual Romanian singer goes viral but sparks criticism
stories.summary: In Romania, an AI-generated singer inspired by poems about the Roma community has become an overnight success. But critics say her creator is monetizing the woes of a marginalized group without involving its members.

stories.title: Hungarians living in Ukraine caught between front lines
stories.summary: About 80,000 ethnic Hungarians live in western Ukraine. Many feel torn between Viktor Orban's anti-Ukrainian campaign and the conflict between the two countries. What does the future hold for this minority?

stories.title: JD Vance criticizes Zelenskyy and Ukraine in Hungary on visit campaigning for Viktor Orban
stories.summary: US Vice President JD Vance has criticized EU leaders and Ukraine's Volodymyr Zelenskyy during his visit to Hungary, echoing Prime Minister Viktor Orban's campaign messaging. The Kremlin chimed in from Moscow, too.

stories.title: Republicans wanted Trump to sell the economy. He’s been talking war instead.
stories.summary: President Donald Trump and his administration were supposed to be well on their way by now to convincing the American people that the U.S. is, indeed, entering a new “golden age.” The Iran war has thrown that plan into shambles. A POLITICO analysis found that the president has spent less time talking about affordability in his public remarks since the war began, and significantly more time talking about the U.S. military and the conflict in the Middle East. Back in January, Trump spoke about aff

stories.title: Ukraine-Russia blame game over Easter ceasefire violations
stories.summary: Ukraine and Russia accused each other of breaching an Orthodox Easter ceasefire hundreds of times within hours of it taking effect. Ukraine’s armed forces recorded 2,299 ceasefire violations as of 7 a.m. Sunday morning, the general staff of Ukraine’s military wrote in a post on Facebook . Russia’s defense ministry, meanwhile, accused the Ukrainian military of breaching the ceasefire 1,971 times by 8 a.m. on Sunday. Russia declared a temporary 32-hour ceasefire to mark Orthodox

stories.title: The MAGA loyalist working to grow the foreign guest-worker program
stories.summary: CAMBRIDGE, Maryland — On Capitol Hill, Rep. Andy Harris is one of the most uncompromising advocates of President Donald Trump’s immigration crackdown. On the Eastern Shore of the Chesapeake Bay, the Maryland Republican is seen as a hero for securing foreign labor to power his state’s commercial seafood industry. The 69-year-old lawmaker, who chairs the ultraconservative House Freedom Caucus and the subcommittee that funds the Department of Agriculture, has leveraged his influence as on

stories.title: Home Office to announce closure of 11 asylum hotels in next week
stories.summary: Exclusive: closures are part of pledge by Labour to end all use of hotels for asylum seekers by end of this parliament The Home Office is to announce the closure of 11 asylum hotels this week as part of its pledge to close all such facilities by the end of this parliament. The use of hotels to house asylum seekers has been controversial since it became widespread at the start of the Covid pandemic. Anti-migrant protesters have staged demonstrations outside hotels, claiming asylum seekers are liv

stories.title: Trump’s new budget ignores dying Americans and gives away record sums to the US military
stories.summary: President’s proposed budget slashes health department by 12% while throwing $1.5tn – a 42% increase – to the military Americans are dying in droves. Deaths due to avoidable causes in the United States –which could be dealt with via prevention or proper healthcare – far outpace those in most of country’s peers in the industrialized world. Most notably, Americans die of treatable conditions at nearly twice the rate as Spaniards, French, Japanese and Australians. They would most likely live longer

stories.title: US and Iran fail to reach agreement after marathon talks
stories.summary: U.S. Vice President JD Vance and other top Trump administration officials are heading home empty handed. Marathon negotiations with Iran in Pakistan this weekend failed to produce a breakthrough that would definitively end the war, which is entering its seventh week. Vance, who spoke early Sunday morning local time, said the Iranians refused to give assurances that they would not try to obtain or develop a nuclear weapon. “They have chosen not to accept our terms,” Vance said. “The simple fact i

stories.title: UK puts Diego Garcia handover on hold to appease Trump
stories.summary: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s government is setting aside a plan to hand over the Chagos Islands to Mauritius because of opposition from U.S. President Donald Trump’s administration, according to British media reports.  A bill underpinning the deal will not be included in the King’s Speech next month, The Times and other outlets reported on Saturday. POLITICO reported in February that Starmer’s administration would “pause for thought” on the Chagos plan. The biggest island

stories.title: New relief for households being considered as Albanese government warns of ‘long tail’ from Iran war
stories.summary: Catherine King says while peace talks were ‘best chance’ at lowering fuel prices, further help may be included in budget Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Track Australia’s fuel prices, service station outages and shipments in charts Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Albanese government is contemplating further relief for struggling households and businesses in next month’s federal budget, as peace talks continue between the US and Iran amid a

stories.title: ‘Never been closer’: UFO watchers buoyed by Trump and Vance’s alien ‘obsession’
stories.summary: From Vance’s interest to Trump’s commitment to disclosure, administration’s fascination with UFOs has experts feeling close to evidence of aliens Like most politicians, Donald Trump did not campaign on the issue of space aliens. But 15 months into his second term, UFO enthusiasts have been buoyed by the Trump administration’s apparent fascination with extraterrestrials, with one expert claiming the human race has “never been closer” to being presented with hard evidence of aliens. After a largel

stories.title: ‘We waited 12 years’: escapees from Syria’s camps face an uncertain future
stories.summary: A young Albanian woman’s escape from al-Hawl offers rare hope – but as the camp empties many are left stranded, prompting urgent calls for repatriation For weeks he hovered near Turkey’s border with Syria hoping for good news. In early February, Xhetan Ndregjoni got word of what he was waiting for – his niece Eva was on her way after escaping the squalid desert camp in Syria where she had been held without charge since she was a child. “I don’t have the words to describe that moment,” Ndregjoni

stories.title: Blue city, red state battle: Kansas City feuds over ‘colonial’ police system
stories.summary: Democratic city does not control its police force – and reform attempts have been thwarted by Republicans Quinton Lucas said he thinks his city’s police department “is a colonial system”. “I think it is anti-Black. I think it is anti-immigrant. I think it is anti-almost everything we stand for in terms of making sure that diverse populations in major cities have a voice in terms of navigating it,” the Kansas City mayor said. Continue reading...

stories.title: Peruvians go to polls hoping to break cycle of instability
stories.summary: Crime and corruption top voter concerns in highly unpredictable election with 35 candidates for president Peruvians go to the polls on Sunday hoping to break a cycle of instability that has produced nine presidents in a decade as well as surging violent crime , corruption scandals and overwhelming distrust in institutions and politicians. About 27 million people who are eligible to vote must choose between a record 35 presidential candidates as well as contenders for the bicameral congress – all

stories.title: Donald Trump and Marco Rubio watch UFC fights in Miami as peace talks with Iran fail
stories.summary: President has long been a fan of mixed martial arts Ivanka and Donald Trump Jr also at event Donald Trump and US secretary of state Marco Rubio attended a UFC event in Miami night on Saturday night as peace talks with Iran failed on the other side of the world. Trump entered the Kaseya Center shortly after 9pm alongside several members of his family and UFC chief Dana White, who has been a supporter of the president since his first term. Seated nearby was Rubio as well as US ambassador to India

stories.title: French man charged with keeping nine-year-old son locked in van since 2024
stories.summary: Police rescued boy after neighbour reported sounds of a child coming from vehicle in Hagenbach in eastern France A malnourished nine-year-old boy was rescued after being locked in his father’s van since 2024 in eastern France, a prosecutor said. A neighbour alerted police to “sounds of a child” coming from a vehicle in the village of Hagenbach, near the borders of Switzerland and Germany. Continue reading...

stories.title: Germany: Betting on elections is illegal — but possible
stories.summary: Online gambling is gaining traction in Germany. Using prediction markets like Polymarket to bet on political events remains illegal, but it is almost impossible to police.

stories.title: Ukraine and Russia swap 175 war prisoners as Easter ceasefire starts
stories.summary: Ukraine and Russia swapped 175 prisoners of war each on Saturday as a 32-hour ceasefire began on the occasion of Orthodox Easter. Ukraine’s President Volodymyr Zelenskyy posted pictures of the Ukrainian soldiers being welcomed back home. The group includes troops who have fought on different war fronts, from Mariupol to the Donetsk, Kharkiv and Zaporizhzhia regions. Seven civilians were included. “Some of them are wounded. Most have been in captivity since 2022. And finally — they are home,” Zel

stories.title: ‘Another institutional abuse’: UK survivor of Irish mother and baby home can’t afford to accept compensation
stories.summary: Rosemary Adaser, 70, would lose £1,000 a month in benefits, while her twin brother in Ireland got full amount with no penalty A twin sister and brother were separated from their parents in Ireland’s mother and baby home scandal – but only one of them can be compensated without being penalised. Rosemary Adaser, 70, of Ealing, west London, was among tens of thousands of children placed in abusive institutions for being born out of marriage in Ireland. Continue reading...

Historic face-to-face meetings in Pakistan – marking the highest-level of direct engagement between Wa

stories.title: A festival of young European photography
stories.summary: The Circulation(s) photo festival has returned to Paris for its 16th edition. On show are works by 26 young European photographers. The projects capture the pulse of young European photography, its intuitions, challenges and commitments Circulation(s) will take place at the Centquatre-Paris from 21 March to 17 May 2026 Continue reading...

stories.title: Floods, power outages and hundreds evacuated as Cyclone Vaianu lashes New Zealand’s North Island
stories.summary: Cyclone crossed coast near Maketu peninsula, packing destructive winds exceeding 130km/h (80 mph), heavy rain and large swells Cyclone Vaianu made landfall in New Zealand’s North Island on Sunday, triggering floods, power outages and forcing hundreds to evacuate . The cyclone crossed the coast near the Maketu peninsula, packing destructive winds exceeding 130km/h (80 mph), heavy rain and large swells, national weather provider MetService said, describing Vaianu as a “life-threatening” system. Co

stories.title: Benin holds presidential election four months after failed coup
stories.summary: As president Patrice Talon steps down after a decade, the west African country’s finance minister is favourite to win This Sunday, just four months after a failed coup, Benin heads to the polls for a presidential election that feels more like a coronation than a contest. Patrice Talon, the businessman turned politician who has been president since 2016, is ineligible to run again after serving two five-year terms. Continue reading...

stories.title: Can’t beet it! Humble mangelwurzel to star at Chelsea flower show
stories.summary: Formerly unloved vegetable casts off lowly roots to feature in Great Pavilion after online craze among young gardeners They are an unloved root vegetable traditionally grown for cattle feed, and when pulled from the ground they look like an ingredient destined for a witch’s cauldron. But the humble mangelwurzel will be in pride of place in the Great Pavilion at this year’s Chelsea flower show (19-23 May), after becoming the subject of an online craze among young gardeners. Continue reading...

stories.title: From brutal occupation to brazen recruitment: Russia turns Bucha residents against their own
stories.summary: Four years after the Ukrainian town experienced some of the war’s worst atrocities, a 21-year-old planted bombs outside his own apartment building On a recent evening in March, Bohdan Tymchenko, a quiet and unassuming man from Bucha, logged on to his computer to play the popular video game World of Tanks. Less than two weeks later, he planted two bombs outside his flat. What unfolded in the intervening days offers a stark glimpse into a growing pattern: Ukrainians drawn in online by Russian inte

stories.title: JD Vance says talks failed due to Iran’s refusal to give up nuclear programme
stories.summary: Iranian delegates say Washington needs to do more to win their trust if talks in Islamabad to resolve US-Iran conflict are to be successful Middle East crisis – live updates The US vice-president, JD Vance, has blamed the failure of marathon negotiations with Iran on the country’s refusal to abandon its nuclear weapons programme, while Iranian delegates have claimed Washington needs to do more to win their trust. stories.title: Remote working tribunal cases in Great Britain fall for first time since Covid
stories.summary: Last year’s drop may reflect rising unemployment and improved right to request flexible working, experts say The number of workers in Great Britain taking their bosses to employment tribunals over remote working fell last year for the first time since Covid hit, with a tightening labour market making some more reluctant to leave roles despite return-to-office mandates . stories.title: At least 30 killed in crush at historic fortress in Haiti
stories.summary: Officials said many killed at popular tourist site were young, with more people reported injured or missing At least 30 people, many of them young, have died and dozens more are reported to have been injured after a crush at a mountaintop fortress in northern Haiti that is a popular tourist spot. stories.summary: Three arrested by federal agents had family ties to Iranian military general, regime spokesperson or security chief United States federal agents arrested three Iranian nationals – including the son of a revolutionary at the center of the 1979 Iran hostage crisis – after the US state department terminated their green cards, the department announced on Saturday. stories.title: What could come after a rules-based world order?
stories.summary: The war between the US, Israel and Iran was, for many, a sign that the rules-based world order, a set of principles developed after World War II, is collapsing. If that era is over, what could be next?

stories.title: Ireland clears refinery blockade as fuel protests trigger nationwide shortages
stories.summary: Authorities used force to reopen the country's only refinery, after days of disruption over petrol prices left fuel pumps dry and threatened emergency services operations.

stories.summary: India is reaching out to its key economic partners in the Middle East to take stock of the impact of the Iran war and ensure smooth trade. Meanwhile, PM Modi is busy campaigning in West Bengal. DW has more.

stories.title: Germany news: Most Germans see decline in public manners
stories.summary: Most Germans think people's manners are getting worse, although most rate themselves as polite. Meawhile, there's trouble at the top of Europe's largest economy amid a dispute over fuel price relief. DW has more.

stories.title: China boosts ties with Taiwan after opposition leader visits

stories.title: India: Legendary playback singer Asha Bhosle dies at 92

stories.title: Haiti: More than 30 killed in stampede at historical fort
stories.summary: A deadly stampede broke out during an annual event to celebrate Citadelle Laferriere, a UNESCO-listed fort that is a symbol of Haiti's hard-won independence.

stories.summary: A Tisza Párt szombaton este Debrecenben tartotta kampányzáróját. A szimpatizánsok szerint, ha a Fidesz nyerne, az emberek biztosan nem néznék tétlenül.

stories.title: First gay rights movement: Berlin's wild 1920s queer history
stories.summary: Before the Nazis took power, Berlin was a center of LGBTQ+ rights and queer culture, with research institutes, a flourishing nightlife and one of the earliest gay rights' movements.

stories.title: Israeli restaurant targeted in suspected antisemitic attack in Munich
stories.summary: Antisemitic offences in Germany have risen significantly in recent years, reaching a new peak in 2024. Authorities recorded 6,236 cases, including 173 violent incidents, according to available data.

stories.title: Mr Nobody on leaving Russia behind: Oscar-winning director Pavel Talankin talks home truths
stories.summary: In an exclusive interview, Paval Talankin, winner of the 2026 Best Documentary Oscar, tells Euronews about live outside his Russian homeland and the "discarded" generation for whom he made 'Mr Nobody Against Putin'.

stories.title: Ongoing wars 'not failure of UN but of member states', General Assembly President tells Euronews
stories.summary: The head of the United Nations' main policy-making organ responded to mounting criticism of her organisation in a Euronews interview, arguing the abuse of vetoes by key members is blocking progress.

stories.title: Former hostage In Iran faces surreal tax troubles in France after release
stories.summary: Former French hostage in Iran, Benjamin Brière, said his release did not end his ordeal, as he struggled with taxes, benefits and reintegration back home.

stories.title: Emmanuel Macron meets Pope Leo XIV at the Vatican to discuss global tensions
stories.summary: France’s President Emmanuel Macron met Pope Leo XIV at the Vatican on Friday, marking their first official talks and signalling renewed dialogue between France and the Holy See at a time of rising global tensions.

stories.title: Israel: Hezbollah rocket from Lebanon strikes Safed, injuring several people
stories.summary: Emergency services were deployed in Safed on 10 April 2026 after a rocket fired from Lebanon by Hezbollah struck the northern city, prompting a rapid response to treat casualties and secure the area.

stories.title: Italy: From Go to Monopoly, board games bridge generations
stories.summary: A new exhibition titled “0–99. Design for Play” opened on 10 April 2026 at Palazzo Arese Borromeo in Cesano Maderno, near Milan, turning a 17th‑century aristocratic residence into a large, interactive gallery of games.

stories.title: JD Vance warns Iran not to 'play' US during planned talks in Pakistan
stories.summary: The US vice president said he was "looking forward to negotiations" in Pakistan as UK Prime Minister Starmer classified the ceasefire as "fragile".

stories.title: Artemis II mission was a triumph - now comes the hard part
stories.summary: The mission was almost flawless but there are considerable obstacles ahead before a Moon landing.

stories.title: This idyllic US town was full of police families - and a serial killer in their midst
stories.summary: For years, rumours swirled about who could be behind the bodies being discovered on the shores of Gilgo Beach.

stories.title: Concerns after satellite provider restricts Iran images following US pressure
stories.summary: Planet Labs says it is restricting coverage of Iran and parts of the Middle East indefinitely.

stories.title: NASA's Artemis II astronauts back on earth after record-breaking trip around the moon
stories.summary: With their flight now complete, the four astronauts have set NASA up for a moon landing by another crew in just two years and a full-blown moon base within the decade.

stories.title: Artemis crew home safely after completing historic mission to the Moon
stories.summary: Four astronauts splashed down in the Pacific after a nine-day voyage that took them further from Earth than any humans.

stories.title: 'You are my god', Japan's PM tells British rock band Deep Purple
stories.summary: Hard rock enthusiast Sanae Takaichi beamed as she met the rockers in her Tokyo office.

stories.title: Djibouti's president wins unprecedented sixth term with 97.8% of vote
stories.summary: Ismail Omar Guelleh, 78, has extended his already 27-year rule in a poll boycotted by the main opposition candidates.

stories.title: Still in shock, Beirut tries to heal after deadly Israeli attacks
stories.summary: BBC Middle East Correspondent Hugo Bachega hears from people affected by this week's attacks.

stories.title: US and Iranian officials begin high-stakes peace talks in Pakistan to resolve war
stories.summary: Three party talks with the US, Iran and Pakistan have begun after a reduction in Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon and other preconditions being met, according to Iran's IRNA news agency.

stories.title: Moment Artemis II capsule splashes down in Pacific
stories.summary: The Artemis II crew splashed down in the Pacific Ocean off the west coast of the United States.

stories.title: Trump ‘reaping bitter fruit’ of thinking Iran intervention as easy as Venezuela, says former diplomat
stories.summary: John Feeley says US president was ‘flush with victory’ of Maduro capture and could make same mistake in Cuba Donald Trump is “reaping the bitter fruit” of erroneously thinking that the capture of Venezuela’s president, Nicolás Maduro, offered a blueprint for toppling the Iranian regime, according to one of the US state department’s most respected former Latin America experts. John Feeley, a Marine helicopter pilot who later served as the US ambassador to Panama, believed Trump had been “flush wi

stories.title: Ukraine and Syria tighten relations
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy's visit to Syria was not only a diplomatic gesture but motivated by strategic considerations regarding Russia, once a strong supporter of the Middle Eastern state.

stories.title: German Bundeswehr says military-aged men can travel freely
stories.summary: A new rule requiring men to get permission to travel abroad recently put Defense Minister Boris Pistorius under pressure. He says he has fixed the situation, but the criticism continues.

stories.title: Ukrainians shot down Iran's drones in the Gulf — what does Kyiv get in return?
stories.summary: Ukraine’s president announced that Ukrainian experts in the Middle East have already shot down Iranian drones in “several countries” with domestically produced interceptors.

stories.title: Druzhba oil pipeline will be fixed this spring, Zelenskyy says
stories.summary: Ukraine’s president said the Druzhba pipeline, whose operational status remains tied to EU funding for Kyiv, will be fixed later this spring, with repair work under way.

stories.title: Wild chimpanzees recorded waging ‘civil war’ with coordinated attacks between two groups
stories.summary: New study describes what may be the first case of a unified community of chimps, in Uganda, turning on itself On a June day in 2015, primatologist Aaron Sandel was quietly observing a small cluster of the Ngogo chimpanzee group in Uganda’s Kibale national park when he noticed something strange. As other members of the chimpanzees’ wider group moved closer through the forest, the chimpanzees in front of him began to display nervous behaviour. They grimaced and touched each other for reassurance,

stories.title: Islamabad prepares to host historic negotiations between Iran and the US
stories.summary: In Pakistan’s capital, the army has been deployed, a public holiday has been declared and the streets are eerily empty Middle East crisis – live updates The streets of Islamabad were on strict lockdown as Pakistan’s capital prepared to play host to historic negotiations between Iran and the US that have dangled the promise of an end to war that has devastated the Middle East. Even as the US-Iran ceasefire looked increasingly precarious , amid Israel’s continued bombardment of Lebanon and dispute

stories.title: Weather tracker: Cyclone Maila batters Solomon Islands with 115mph winds
stories.summary: Powerful storm brings destruction, while temperatures soar in Vietnam and torrential rain lashes South Korea Severe Tropical Cyclone Maila, currently in the Solomon Sea, is expected to continue moving south-westwards over the coming days. According to the Australian Bureau of Meteorology, Maila had peak sustained winds of 115mph (185km/h), with gusts up to 160mph on Thursday, making it the strongest cyclone recorded this far north in the Solomon Sea. The storm has caused widespread damage across

stories.title: Ukraine: Russia's Putin declares Easter ceasefire
stories.summary: Russian President Putin has announced a ceasefire in its war in Ukraine over the Orthodox Easter holiday. Ukrainian President Zelenskyy said his country will "act accordingly" during the truce.

stories.title: Lufthansa cabin crew begin strike
stories.summary: Hundreds of flights have been canceled as cabin crew members walk out. This is Lufthansa's third major strike this year.

stories.title: Recycling's next big thing — or big bluff?
stories.summary: An advanced recycling process claims it can handle hard-to-recycle plastics, like packaging. But critics say it's not worth the effort.

stories.title: Germany: Police suspect antisemitism in Israeli restaurant attack in Munich
stories.summary: Police and restaurant owners believe antisemitism motivated the Munich attack. Nobody was hurt, but three windows were broken, causing damage worth several thousand euros. This comes amid tension over the war in Iran.

stories.title: NASA's billion-dollar space race goes into overdrive
stories.summary: NASA's renewed push to get a human mission to the moon and Mars is neither cheap, nor for the faint of heart. But who ends up covering the costs and what does everyone still on Earth get out of all this?

stories.title: UAE urges full compliance from Iran following ceasefire announcement
stories.summary: The UAE is seeking clarifications on the US-Iran ceasefire deal regarding attacks across the Gulf, as well as on the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz as a “central priority”, as Tehran's blockade continues despite its commitment to reopen the strait to free navigation.

stories.title: Trump again berates NATO, calls it 'disappointing'
stories.summary: Trump's outrage at NATO allies over their failure to join the Iran war had prompted fears he would seek to pull the United States out of the nearly eight-decade-old alliance.

stories.title: Germany has resumed diplomatic talks with Iran, Chancellor Friedrich Merz says
stories.summary: Merz also said that the continued Israeli military campaign in Lebanon could jeopardise peace talks expected between the United States and Iran over the war in the Middle East.

stories.title: Cuba accuses US of 'extorting' Latin America by forcing countries to cancel doctors deals
stories.summary: According to official figures, some 24,000 Cuban doctors and other healthcare professionals were deployed in 56 countries in 2025.

stories.title: Melania Trump: 'I never had a relationship with Epstein'
stories.summary: Watch the first lady's full statement from the White House as she addresses her links to the late sex offender.
